title: sarcocornia perennis subsp. alpini
intro: Sarcocornia perennis subsp. alpini, also known as shrubby glasswort, is a perennial succulent plant that belongs to the family Amaranthaceae. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Sarcocornia perennis subsp. alpini include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant activity. In herbal preparations, it can be consumed as a decoction or infusion, or applied topically as a paste or ointment, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses.
